mission.title: urunc: Create a document for Knative and urunc
mission.description: Create a document for Knative and urunc. Requested by 0+ users.

steps.title: Check current urunc deployment
steps.description:
Verify your urunc version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n urunc -l app.kubernetes.io/name=urunc
helm list -n urunc 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working urunc installation.

steps.title: Review urunc configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant urunc configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n urunc -l app.kubernetes.io/name=urunc
kubectl get configmap -n urunc -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=urunc
```
We should also add a section in our documentation regarding the integration of urunc with Knative. The document should be similar to the existing k8s document.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Create a document for Knative and urunc
steps.description:
Hello  

indeed building unikernels is not very straightforward.That is the reason we created [bunny](https://github.com/nubificus/bunny). However, currently `bunny` is not able to build unikernels from scratch, but we are working on it. However, our documentation could definitely get improved and
```yaml
git clone https://github.com/unikraft/catalog
cd catalog/examples/http-python3.12-flask3.0
kraft build .

[●] Build completed successfully!
 │
 └─ initramfs: .unikraft/build/initramfs-x86_64.cpio (52 MB)
```

steps.title: Upgrade urunc to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade urunc:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade urunc urunc/urunc --namespace urunc
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n urunc
helm list -n urunc
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n urunc -l app.kubernetes.io/name=urunc
kubectl get events -n urunc --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Create a document for Knative and urunc" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Hello  

indeed building unikernels is not very straightforward.That is the reason we created [bunny](https://github.com/nubificus/bunny). However, currently `bunny` is not able to build unikernels from scratch, but we are working on it.
codeSnippets:
git clone https://github.com/unikraft/catalog
cd catalog/examples/http-python3.12-flask3.0
kraft build .

[●] Build completed successfully!
 │
 └─ initramfs: .unikraft/build/initramfs-x86_64.cpio (52 MB)

#syntax=harbor.nbfc.io/nubificus/pun:0.1.0
FROM scratch

COPY build/app-nginx_qemu-x86_64 /unikernel/kernel
COPY data.cpio /unikernel/initrd

LABEL com.urunc.unikernel.binary=/unikernel/kernel
LABEL "com.urunc.unikernel.initrd"=/unikernel/initrd
LABEL "com.urunc.unikernel.cmdline"='nginx -c /nginx/conf/nginx.conf'
LABEL "com.urunc.unikernel.unikernelType"="unikraft"
LABEL "com.urunc.unikernel.hypervisor"="qemu"

#syntax=harbor.nbfc.io/nubificus/bunny:latest
version: v0.1

#syntax=harbor.nbfc.io/nubificus/bunny:latest
version: v0.1

platforms:
  framework: unikraft
  monitor: qemu
  architecture: x86

rootfs:
  from: local # specifies that the path is in the local build context
  path: .unikraft/build/initramfs-x86_64.cpio

kernel:
  from: unikraft.org/python:3.12
  path: /unikraft/bin/kernel

cmdline: "/app/server.py"

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with urunc installed or the issue environment reproducible.
